qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated remote code execution via test script execution endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same insecure default configuration vulnerability described in the advisory. The advisory describes that 'secureEnabled' flag is commented out by default in server/settings.default.js, causing authentication to be disabled. The finding correctly identifies this root cause, noting that verifyGroups() returns admin-level permission (-1) when secureEnabled is false, and demonstrates a concrete exploit path leveraging the default insecure configuration to achieve RCE through the /api/runscript endpoint.

selected_finding.title: Unauthenticated remote code execution via test script execution endpoint

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The rce-via-test-script finding correctly identifies the advisory-described insecure default configuration vulnerability in FUXA v1.2.7. The finding describes the same root cause: secureEnabled is disabled by default in server/settings.default.js, causing verifyGroups() to return admin-level permissions without authentication. The finding provides detailed code locations and exploit path demonstrating how this insecure default enables unauthenticated RCE through the /api/runscript endpoint. However, the PoC execution failed during Docker build due to network timeout when pulling the base image, preventing verification of end-to-end exploit success or bug activation. The finding is semantically correct and matches the advisory, but lacks runtime evidence due to environment setup failure.
evidence: Finding identifies the same vulnerable file server/settings.default.js where secureEnabled is commented out by default
Finding correctly describes verifyGroups() returning admin-level permission (-1) when secureEnabled is false, matching the advisory's missing authentication issue
Finding provides concrete code locations in server/api/index.js:184-194, server/api/scripts/index.js:33-55, and server/runtime/scripts/msm.js:99-145
Finding demonstrates exploit path from disabled authentication to RCE through /api/runscript endpoint
PoC reproduction bundle exists but Docker build failed due to network connectivity issues
blockers_to_higher_tier: Docker build failed during base image pull (network timeout to docker.io/auth.docker.io)
No runtime evidence of exploit success or bug activation was captured
Could not verify that the PoC triggers the advisory-described vulnerable code path

poc_executions.observed_effect: Docker build failed due to network timeout when attempting to pull node:18-bookworm base image. The failure occurred during the Docker build phase before the vulnerable application could be instantiated or the exploit payload executed.
